buster.def: 打碎者、破壞者、(口)小孩、破壞的人的人、破壞地 (破)
buster.col: ghost buster, bronco buster, block buster
buster.ex: The movie was a global block buster that year.

bustle.def: 忙亂、喧囂、奔忙、充滿、忙亂的人、忙亂地 (忙)
bustle.ex: The market was starting to bustle with early morning activity.

bustling.def: 喧囂的、忙亂的、熱鬧的人的人 (或物) (鬧)
bustling.ex: New York is a bustling city that never sleeps.

bust-up/bustup.ex: They had a major bust-up over the weekend.

busy.def: 忙碌的、熱鬧的、忙於、使忙碌、使熱鬧的的人、忙碌地 (忙)
busy.col: busy as a bee, get busy, keep busy, busy schedule
busy.ex: He is very busy preparing for his exams.

busybody.ex: The local busybody is always poking her nose into others' business.

busyness.ex: The busyness of modern life can be very stressful.

but.def: 但是、可是、除...之外、僅僅、(感嘆詞)可是！、但是的人物、但是地 (但是)
but.col: all but (幾乎), anything but (絕不), but for (要不是)
but.ex: I'm sorry, but I can't come to the party tonight.

butch.def: (俚)壯碩的、有男子氣概的人的女人、粗魯的人的人物、壯碩地 (壯)
butch.ex: She looked quite butch in her leather jacket and short hair.

butcher.def: 屠夫、肉商、屠殺、弄壞、肉店、屠殺的人物、屠殺地 (屠)
butcher.col: family butcher, butcher a song (唱得極難聽)
butcher.ex: The local butcher only sells the highest quality meat.

butchering.ex: Butchering such a beautiful animal was a tragedy.

butchery.def: 肉店、屠宰業、殘殺行為 (單數)
butchery.ex: The war was characterized by senseless butchery.

butler.def: 男管家、(美)家僕、侍候、管家的人的人物 (管家)
butler.ex: The loyal butler has served the family for thirty years.

butt.def: 粗大的一端、菸蒂、屁股、笑柄、用頭撞、菸蒂的人物、碰地 (端)
butt.col: butt in (插嘴), butt of a joke, cigarette butt
butt.ex: He threw the cigarette butt onto the pavement.

butter.def: 奶油、黃油、塗奶油於、奉承、奶油的人的人、奶油地 (奶油)
butter.col: butter someone up (奉承), bread and butter (生計), cocoa butter
butter.ex: I like to spread plenty of butter on my toast.

buttercup.ex: The meadow was filled with bright yellow buttercups.

butterfingers.def: (口)笨手笨腳者、常掉東西的人 (單數)
butterfingers.ex: I dropped the plate; I'm such a butterfingers today!

butterfly.def: 蝴蝶、蝶式、輕佻的人、心慌意亂的人物、蝴蝶地 (蝴蝶)
butterfly.col: butterflies in one's stomach (緊張), social butterfly
butterfly.ex: A colorful butterfly landed on the garden flower.

buttermilk.ex: Buttermilk is often used in baking pancakes and biscuits.

buttery.def: 像奶油的、奉承的、貯藏室、奶油地 (奶油)
buttery.ex: The fresh croissants have a delicious buttery flavor.

buttock.ex: He was injured in the left buttock during the climb.

button.def: 鈕扣、按鈕、扣上、按、鈕扣的人的人、扣上地 (扣)
button.col: push the button, button up, belly button
button.ex: I need to sew a button back onto my shirt.

buttoned.def: 扣上鈕扣的、沈默寡言的人的人 (或物)
buttoned.ex: Wait until you're properly buttoned up before going out.

buttonhole.def: 鈕扣孔、(英)襟花、攔住...說話的人、襟花地 (孔)
buttonhole.ex: He wore a white carnation in his buttonhole.

buttoning.def: 扣鈕扣、扣上的人 (或物) (扣)
buttoning.ex: He was still buttoning his jacket as he ran out.

buttress.def: 扶壁、支撐物、支持、支撐的人的人、支持地 (支撐)
buttress.ex: The flying buttresses support the heavy walls of the cathedral.

buxom.def: (女)體態豐滿的、健康的、快樂地 (豐滿)
buxom.ex: The shop was run by a friendly, buxom woman.

buy.def: 買、收買、相信、划算的東西、買的人的、購買地 (買)
buy.col: buy out, buy into, buy off (收買), buy time
buy.ex: I need to buy some groceries for the week.

buyer.ex: They haven't found a potential buyer for the house yet.

buying.def: 買、購買、採購的人 (或物) (買)
buying.ex: The increase in taxes reduced the people's buying power.

buzz.def: 嗡嗡聲、(口)流言、興奮、嗡嗡叫、忙碌、嗡嗡的人、忙碌地 (嗡)
buzz.col: buzz around, catch a buzz, give someone a buzz (打電話)
buzz.ex: I heard a low buzz from the electrical box.

buzzard.ex: A circling buzzard was searching for prey in the valley.

buzzer.def: 蜂鳴器、喊話器、嗡嗡叫的人 (單數)
buzzer.ex: Please press the door buzzer when you arrive.

buzzing.def: 嗡嗡叫的、熱鬧的的人的人 (或物) (鳴)
buzzing.ex: The room was buzzing with excitement and talk.

buzzword.ex: 'Sustainability' has become a popular buzzword in business.

by.def: 在...旁邊、經由、被、由於、在近處、經過、在近處的人、在旁地 (旁)
by.col: by and by (不久), by the way, by all means, by far
by.ex: The supermarket is right by the train station.

bye-bye.def: (口)再見、小孩子的人 (或物) (拜拜)

by-election/byelection.ex: There will be a parliamentary by-election next month.

by-gone/bygone.def: 過去的、以前的、往事、舊帳的人、往事地 (往)
by-gone/bygone.col: let bygones be bygones (既往不咎), bygone era
by-gone/bygone.ex: The museum displays artifacts from a bygone era.

by-law/bylaw.ex: The company amended its administrative by-laws.

by-name/byname.ex: Richard I is often referred to by his by-name, 'Lionheart'.

bypass.def: 繞道、旁道、(醫)分流術、繞過、避開、繞過的人、繞過地 (繞)
bypass.col: road bypass, heart bypass, bypass surgery
bypass.ex: The new bypass will reduce traffic in the town center.

bypassing.ex: Bypassing the standard procedure saved them three weeks.
